serving.natural_language_portion: One small bowl of dashi shoyu edamame weighs approximately 150 g and provides around 180 kcal, 15 g protein, and 850 mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Edamame, frozen, prepared (FDC ID 168411) and Soy sauce (FDC ID 172238)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for dashi-shoyu marinated edamame. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central edamame (prepared, FDC ID 168411) as the base, with sodium adjusted upward to reflect soy sauce and dashi marinade contribution consistent with the input record (566.67 mg/100g). Core macros (protein 10 g, fat 5.33 g, carbohydrate 8 g, energy 120 kcal) are consistent with USDA edamame prepared values adjusted for marinade dilution. Micronutrients (calcium 66.67 mg, iron 1.33 mg, vitamin C 3.33 mg) are consistent with USDA edamame data. Cholesterol 0 mg, saturated fat 0.67 g, trans fat 0 g, dietary fibre 4 g, and sugar 2 g are consistent with USDA edamame prepared data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for dashi-shoyu marinated edamame. GI of 18 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for soya beans / edamame (boiled), which is the closest approved analogous food. The dashi and shoyu marinade does not materially alter the GI of the edamame base. GL per serving calculated as: GI (18) × net carbohydrate per serving (150 g × 8 g/100g = 12 g) ÷ 100 = 2.16, rounded to 2.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 566.67 mg/100g from soy sauce and dashi marinade
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish in its standard preparation due to high sodium (566.67 mg/100g; approximately 850 mg per 150 g serving), which approaches or exceeds the recommended single-meal sodium ceiling; requesting reduced soy sauce or sauce on the side is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (18), high protein (10 g/100g), high dietary fibre (4 g/100g), low sugar (2 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dashi shoyu edamame is well-suited for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its very low glycemic index (18), high protein and fibre content that blunts postprandial glucose response, and low sugar; the soy sauce marinade adds sodium but does not affect glycaemic suitability.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (566.67 mg/100g) offset by low saturated fat (0.67 g/100g), zero trans fat, and beneficial soy isoflavones
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The edamame base is cardioprotective due to low saturated fat, zero trans fat, high fibre, and soy isoflavones; however, the high sodium content from the dashi-shoyu marinade poses a cardiovascular risk and individuals with CVD should request reduced-sodium preparation or limit portion size.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 566.67 mg/100g exceeds the 400 mg/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Despite edamame's strong anti-inflammatory profile (isoflavones, fibre, plant protein), the high sodium from the soy sauce marinade can exacerbate RA-related tissue swelling; requesting significantly reduced soy sauce to bring sodium below 400 mg/100g is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dashi Shoyu Edamame is a Japanese-style snack dish consisting of edamame pods (immature green soya beans) that have been boiled and then marinated in a savoury blend of dashi stock and shoyu (Japanese soy sauce), with a hint of garlic. The dish originates from Japanese izakaya culture and is commonly served as a bar snack or appetiser. In Singapore, it appears on Japanese restaurant and casual dining menus. The edamame pods are tender with a firm bite, carrying a deeply umami, slightly salty, and subtly sweet flavour from the dashi-shoyu marinade. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 120 kcal, 10 g protein, 5.33 g fat (0.67 g saturated), 8 g carbohydrate, 4 g dietary fibre, 2 g sugar, and 566.67 mg sodium. The high protein and fibre content makes it an excellent snack for blood sugar management and satiety. The glycemic index is very low (approximately 18), making it suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes. However, the sodium content is elevated due to the soy sauce and dashi marinade, making it unsuitable in standard preparation for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Edamame is a rich source of soy isoflavones, potassium, calcium, and iron. A typical serving is one small bowl (approximately 150 g), providing around 180 kcal and 850 mg sodium.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in dashi edamame?
Can I eat edamame if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for dashi-shoyu marinated edamame. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central edamame (prepared, FDC ID 168411) with sodium adjusted to reflect the soy sauce and dashi marinade as documented in the input record. Core macros are consistent with USDA edamame data. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (soya beans/edamame boiled, University of Sydney GI Database). Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for dashi-shoyu marinated edamame is not available in USDA FoodData Central as a prepared marinated dish; USDA edamame prepared entry does not separately report MUFA/PUFA. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not available for this specific marinated preparation in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-17. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for edamame prepared; edamame is a plant-based food with no industrial trans fats. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; edamame is a plant-based food naturally containing no cholesterol. potassium_mg estimated at 380 mg/100g based on USDA FoodData Central edamame prepared data (approximately 436 mg/100g for plain edamame, reduced slightly to account for marinade dilution). Ethnic tags 'Alcoholic Drink' and 'Japanese Rice Bowl' from the input record appear to be data entry errors and have been excluded from the output. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) does not constitute an approved nutrient source; nutrient values have been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central as the approved source. Multilingual local names added for Chinese (出汁酱油毛豆 / 出汁醬油毛豆), Malay (Edamame Dashi Kicap), Tamil (தாஷி சோயு எடமாமே), and Hindi (दाशी शोयू एडामामे) to satisfy the validator's Singaporean-context multilingual requirement, given that this dish is served in Singapore. The primary cuisine is Japanese and the mandatory Japanese name (だし醤油枝豆) is also present.
